page: Microsoft Azure D2s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, running on a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor clocked at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 8.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 4.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU includes an L3 cache of 48 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 1 MB respectively. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance at 1888.79 and Geekbench single-core score at 1558.0, fall within the average range. However, multi-core and memory-bound workloads show significant limitations. The stress-ng multi-core performance of 1930.87 and Geekbench multi-core score of 1819.0 are categorized as poor, placing in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 14.75 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 13.25 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 10%.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the D2s_v5 is suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, or small-scale development environments where high multi-core throughput is not required. It exhibits poor performance in database operations with a PassMark database score of 1035.51 and Redis SET operations at 1.93 million operations per second, making it less optimal for high-throughput transactional databases. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable baseline performance for entry-level general-purpose tasks, though resource-intensive applications will face bottlenecks due to the low multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2s_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical core of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, a 48 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: while single-core execution achieves average marks, multi-core and memory-intensive operations perform poorly, placing in the bottom 10%. Specifically, memory bandwidth and multi-core Geekbench scores are highly constrained. This server is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and development environments, but is not recommended for database operations or high-throughput multi-threaded tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2s_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM featuring 2 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor and 8 GB RAM. It offers average single-core performance but exhibits poor multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2s_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated single-core performance for lightweight workloads and basic development environments.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor clocked at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 48 MB L3 cache
Delivers average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Exhibits poor multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth in benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and single-threaded tasks
